title: Conventions of Space and Time

jokes.text: But it will probably be London during the Blitz.
jokes.setup: Troy expresses excitement about Inspector Spacetime's ability to go anywhere in time and space
jokes.punchline: Abed's deadpan observation that despite infinite possibilities, the show predictably returns to WWII London
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific observation about sci-fi tropes. Abed's analytical nature shines through with economic delivery that undercuts Troy's enthusiasm with genre awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And yet somehow we always end up in World War II London.

jokes.text: Do we want him to freak out and then we have to rescue him from some fantasy world where submarines are small enough to enter the bloodstream?
jokes.setup: Britta explaining why they need to ease Abed into their relationship
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific reference to 'Fantastic Voyage' as Abed's potential breakdown scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that's perfectly in character for both Britta's overthinking and the show's meta-awareness of Abed's psychological patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We need to ease Abed into this. If we just tell him we're dating, he might have a complete breakdown. I'm talking full Fantastic Voyage scenario—he'll shrink down to microscopic size and get injected into someone's bloodstream.

jokes.text: I totally want to do that.
jokes.setup: Britta's elaborate fantasy rescue scenario
jokes.punchline: Troy's immediate enthusiasm for the ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Troy's childlike enthusiasm cuts through Britta's neurosis with perfect timing and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yes! Finally! I've been waiting for this my whole life!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What do you need me to do? Do I get a cape? Can I have a cape?

jokes.text: I haven't seen you in over 12 hours.
jokes.setup: Britta casually greeting Abed in the morning
jokes.punchline: Abed's robotic precision in tracking exactly how long it's been since he saw her
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Abed's analytical nature and social awkwardness in one precise line.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Hey Abed, what's up?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: 14 hours and 37 minutes since I last saw you. You got a haircut.

jokes.text: I know you two are having sex. I've known for weeks.
jokes.setup: Troy and Britta's elaborate attempts to hide their relationship
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact revelation that he's known all along
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of the sneaking-around plot with Abed's characteristic directness cutting through the pretense.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I've known about you two for three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What? How?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're not as subtle as you think. Also, I have access to the security camera footage.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: There are security cameras in our dorm?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: There are security cameras everywhere. I'm just the only one who watches them.

jokes.text: I love doughnuts.
jokes.setup: Tense moment after Abed reveals he knows about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Complete topic change to doughnuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his way of diffusing tension through complete non-sequitur, showing emotional intelligence through apparent randomness.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know about you two.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Anyway, has anyone seen those Boston cream doughnuts? They had them in the cafeteria this morning.

jokes.text: For an Inspector Spacetime convention, I would need a bag to put over my head.
jokes.setup: Annie pointing out Jeff doesn't need sunglasses for the convention
jokes.punchline: Jeff's embarrassment about being seen at a sci-fi convention
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Jeff's vanity and social anxiety about being associated with nerd culture.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You don't need sunglasses, Jeff. We're indoors.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not worried about the sun, Annie. I'm worried about someone seeing me here.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: At a sci-fi convention?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: At a place where grown men are dressed as Klingons. Yes.

jokes.text: Why can't I go to a Captain Warptime Convention?
jokes.setup: Pierce wanting to join the group's trip
jokes.punchline: Getting the name of the show completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce moment - shows his disconnect from the group's interests through predictable name-mangling.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I want to go on your trip. What do you call yourselves again?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We're just a study group.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, so I'll be joining Studying Group on their adventure!

jokes.text: You know, in Ancient Greece, it was considered an honor to invite a person my age to a space convention?
jokes.setup: Pierce defending his right to be included
jokes.punchline: Completely anachronistic reference to Ancient Greece having space conventions
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Pierce logic - his tendency to make up historical 'facts' applied to completely modern concepts.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I belong here! Back in Ancient Greece, they had space conventions too, you know. Yeah, that's right - the Spartans were launching shuttles while you people were still figuring out the wheel!

jokes.text: Wait, was there a female inspector? Yes. And everyone hates her. Not because they're sexist. Because she sucks.
jokes.setup: Britta asking about female representation in Inspector Spacetime
jokes.punchline: The defensive clarification that the hatred isn't sexist
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on fandom defensiveness about female characters, delivered with typical Troy/Abed synchronicity.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The Inspector has had several female companions throughout the years.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, and we don't hate them because they're women. We hate them because they're poorly written.

jokes.text: You're on your own, Al Capone.
jokes.setup: Annie leaving after the female Inspector discussion
jokes.punchline: Random rhyming dismissal using Al Capone
jokes.explanation: Cute Annie moment but fairly lightweight - just a quirky exit line that fits her character.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Al Capone? More like Al Ca-GONE!

jokes.text: Because they've never seen a man who's had sex before?
jokes.setup: Annie asking why people are staring at Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's arrogant assumption about convention attendees
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff arrogance - both insulting the convention crowd and bragging about his sexual experience.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why is everyone staring at you?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because I'm the most attractive person here.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We're at a comic book convention.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly.

jokes.text: You bear a striking resemblance to Inspector Spacetime's Supervillain Thoraxis.
jokes.setup: Jeff wondering why people are staring
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he looks like the show's villain
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup that explains the staring and sets up Jeff's discomfort throughout the episode.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why is everyone staring at me?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, you look exactly like The Dark Elf from Game of Thrones.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? No, I don't.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You really do. It's uncanny.

jokes.text: This is the first time I've stayed in a hotel without a number in its name.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining this is her first adult vacation
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about hotel quality as marker of adulthood
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Annie's sheltered background with a specific, relatable detail about cheap hotels.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: This is my first adult vacation. You know how I know I'm an adult? I'm staying at a hotel with a continental breakfast.

jokes.text: I will enjoy the... Jeff won. I know.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to respond to Jeff's dismissive 'enjoy the nerds'
jokes.punchline: Her inability to come up with a comeback and admitting defeat
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Annie's self-awareness and Jeff's ability to get under her skin.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I will! And... and you'll enjoy being... tall and stupid!

jokes.text: I sent him 700 pounds and a plane ticket, so he should be here.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his online friend Toby almost couldn't come from Nigeria
jokes.punchline: Obvious Nigerian prince scam setup
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the Nigerian scam reveal - works because it shows Abed's social naivety despite his media savviness.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My friend Toby almost couldn't make it from Nigeria.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Apparently he's a prince and needs help transferring money out of the country.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I told him I'd wire him my entire savings account, but then I realized—that's probably not how it works.

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe how many people I emailed asking for help, and they completely ignored me.
jokes.setup: Toby thanking Abed for the money
jokes.punchline: The ironic complaint about people ignoring Nigerian prince emails
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the scam setup - the audience knows why people ignore those emails while Abed remains oblivious.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Thanks for the money, Abed.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: You know, it's funny. Everyone ignores those Nigerian prince emails, but when a real Nigerian prince asks for money, suddenly I'm the weird one.

jokes.text: She's such a... Both: Minerva.
jokes.setup: Toby complaining about his fake friend's wife
jokes.punchline: Troy joining in with the Inspector Spacetime reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's fan knowledge while building the fake friendship between Toby and his non-existent friend.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: His wife is always complaining about something. She's never happy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, she's like the Blorgons from Inspector Spacetime — always invading and ruining everything.

jokes.text: What a bitch. Wait, was that the female inspector?
jokes.setup: Everyone calling someone 'Minerva'
jokes.punchline: Britta's realization she might have just insulted the female Inspector
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier female Inspector discussion, showing Britta's social consciousness kicking in.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, Minerva? That's a girl's name, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I just called the Inspector a girl. She's gonna think I was being sexist.

jokes.text: Now, remember, we're just a couple of Captain Warphead fans. Nobody will even notice we're here.
jokes.setup: Annie planning their stealth infiltration
jokes.punchline: Getting the show name wrong while trying to blend in
jokes.explanation: Nice irony of trying to blend in while demonstrating complete ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone needs to blend in. Act natural. We're just here to watch... uh... The Office?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Annie, that's not even a real show.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I know that! I was testing you!

jokes.text: Will you come with us? Where? Why? When?
jokes.setup: Shirley and Annie trying to blend in as fans
jokes.punchline: Annie's panicked barrage of questions when approached
jokes.explanation: Shows Annie's nervous energy and their fish-out-of-water status at the convention.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, what? No, no, we're huge fans! I've seen every episode! Well, not every episode, but like... a lot of episodes. Which episodes have you seen? Do you have a favorite character? Mine changes weekly. Is that normal? Do real fans have a favorite character or do they love everyone equally? Oh God, I'm sweating. Why am I sweating? Do I look nervous? I'm not nervous!

jokes.text: My husband doesn't do carbs. But he would like a scotch.
jokes.setup: Annie ordering room service while pretending to be married to Jeff
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of being health-conscious about carbs but wanting alcohol
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Annie's inexperience with adult relationships and dietary preferences.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Yes, hello, I'd like to order dinner for two. No, no carbs please, we're watching our figure. Oh, and a bottle of your finest wine. Actually, make it two bottles.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What? I'm being health-conscious and romantic!

jokes.text: The good kind.
jokes.setup: Room service asking what kind of scotch Jeff would prefer
jokes.punchline: Annie's vague, unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Shows Annie's lack of sophistication about alcohol while maintaining her fantasy.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The kind that makes you forget you're in a hotel room with a girl who ironed her jeans.

jokes.text: I don't think I like your -ist.
jokes.setup: Shirley defending their right to be at the convention
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay on all the '-ist' discriminations
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both funny and shows Shirley's ability to turn social justice awareness into humor.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We have every right to be here at this convention. We're not sexist, we're not racist, we're not ageist, we're not heightist—we're just very specific about our preferences.

jokes.text: That dude is trying to steal your boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Troy worrying about Abed and Toby's connection
jokes.punchline: Britta applying romantic relationship logic to their friendship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Britta's relationship experience helps her read the Troy/Abed dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You're worried they're gonna break up? Troy, they're not in a relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know, but—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, see, that's your problem. You're applying relationship logic to a friendship. It doesn't work that way. When two people are just friends, they can drift apart without it being a tragedy. It's actually healthy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But what if they stop talking completely?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Then they'll reconnect later, or they won't. Either way, you don't get to have feelings about it because you're not involved. You're like a relationship therapist trying to save a marriage that doesn't exist.

jokes.text: What if they get hit by a car or fall down a well?
jokes.setup: Britta giving advice about 'if you love someone, set them free'
jokes.punchline: Troy's literal interpretation with specific disaster scenarios
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - taking romantic advice and immediately jumping to physical danger scenarios with childlike concern.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So if I love someone, I just... let them go? What if they get hit by a bus? What if they join a cult? What if they become a bus that joins a cult?

jokes.text: How many clipboards you got? Raise the klaxon. Me lorry's plunging it into the Thames, methinks.
jokes.setup: Pierce being asked about his thoughts on the Inspector Spacetime pilot
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical pseudo-British response
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce moment - trying to sound sophisticated but ending up completely incoherent.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I'll tell you what. The production values are top-notch, the writing is extraordinarily clever, and the lead actor has real gravitas. But here's the thing - and I say this with all due respect - I haven't the faintest idea what's going on. Not a clue. It's like watching Shakespeare performed by aliens who learned English phonetically.

jokes.text: Bow before Thoraxis.
jokes.setup: Jeff being mistaken for the villain Thoraxis
jokes.punchline: Fans demanding he perform the villain's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag of Jeff being mistaken for Thoraxis, creating ongoing discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Fan 1
dialogue.line: Do the thing! Do the Thoraxis thing!

dialogue.speaker: Fan 2
dialogue.line: Yeah! Say the line!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Fan 1
dialogue.line: Come on, Thoraxis! 'Bite my shiny metal...!'

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not Thoraxis, I'm just a guy at a convention.

dialogue.speaker: Multiple Fans
dialogue.line: DO IT! DO IT! DO IT!

jokes.text: It's Troy! You know it's Troy! Okay, it's the first part of Troy and Abed.
jokes.setup: Toby pretending not to remember Troy's name
jokes.punchline: Troy's increasingly desperate attempts to make Toby remember him
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Troy's insecurity and the importance of the Troy/Abed dynamic to his identity.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey Toby, it's me, Troy! Troy Barnes!

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Sorry, do I know you?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We met in Spanish class? I'm on the football team? I do impressions?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Doesn't ring a bell.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I sang 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' in the study room! You were there!

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Nope.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Okay, remember when I dressed up as a dinosaur? Or when I got shot with a paintball? Or when I—

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I really don't know who you are.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But... but I'm Troy! I'm in your life! I exist! I'm REAL!

jokes.text: Toby and Abed in the Morning? That's ridiculous. I'm not psycho!
jokes.setup: Toby suggesting a new morning show pairing
jokes.punchline: Troy's defensive reaction and immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's jealousy while referencing the show's recurring Troy and Abed bit.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'm thinking we do a morning show pairing. You and Abed.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What? No. Abed and I have nothing in common.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We're completely different people.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We both like TV and film.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Okay, well, besides that obvious thing, we have nothing in common.

jokes.text: In England, Cadbury creme eggs are sold year-round. I thought that was just a legend.
jokes.setup: Toby mentioning London's appeal
jokes.punchline: Troy's amazement at this mundane 'legend'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - treating a minor candy availability difference like mythological knowledge.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: London is amazing. It's got Big Ben, it's got the Crown Jewels, it's got this legend...

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What legend?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: The Underground.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's just a subway system.

jokes.text: It's for my husband. He really needs the diffuser for his hair. It sounds shallow, but he's a lawyer.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining the hair dryer request
jokes.punchline: Connecting Jeff's vanity to his profession
jokes.explanation: Good character insight connecting Jeff's vanity to professional necessity in a believable way.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, why do you need a hair dryer? Your hair is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Thank you, Annie. A lawyer's appearance is everything.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So you're saying your hair is part of your legal strategy?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Objection: assumes facts not in evidence. But yes.

jokes.text: Hey, instead of this constable, what about a blonde with long legs and a tennis racket?
jokes.setup: Shirley giving thoughtful feedback about staying true to the show
jokes.punchline: Moderator's incredibly shallow counter-suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good satire of focus group mentality and Hollywood's tendency to sexualize everything.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I think the show should stay true to what makes it special. It's about these characters and their genuine connections.

dialogue.speaker: Focus Group Moderator
dialogue.line: That's great, but what if we just made everything more explosive? Like, literally. Explosions in every scene.

jokes.text: Wait, Nigel is married? That is so Thoraxis.
jokes.setup: Revelation that the actor playing Thoraxis is married
jokes.punchline: Fan applying villain characteristics to the actor's personal life
jokes.explanation: Good fan behavior satire - conflating actor with character in a way that makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Convention Attendee
dialogue.line: Wait, Thoraxis is married? To a real person?

dialogue.speaker: Convention Attendee
dialogue.line: That means his wife is married to a villain who destroys entire civilizations.

dialogue.speaker: Convention Attendee
dialogue.line: She probably can't even keep a houseplant alive around him.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go upstairs, towel off, and pray to God that I wake up in the middle of a final for a class I didn't know I was enrolled in.
jokes.setup: Jeff dealing with the chaos of being mistaken for Thoraxis
jokes.punchline: Preferring a classic anxiety dream to current reality
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff line - sophisticated reference to anxiety dreams while showing how overwhelmed he is by nerd culture.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I'd rather be back in one of those classic anxiety dreams where I show up to class naked and unprepared. At least then I'd wake up and realize none of it was real. This? This is real, and I'm still not prepared.

jokes.text: A quantum spanner. Yeah, it lights up or plays music or something.
jokes.setup: Britta giving Troy a gift to cheer him up
jokes.punchline: Her complete lack of knowledge about what she bought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - good intentions but complete cluelessness about the subject matter.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I got you something to cheer you up! It's a limited edition collectible action figure.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Britta, this is a My Little Pony doll.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Yeah, and? It's limited edition. The guy at the store said so.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You bought this at a toy store, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, I bought it at a very sophisticated collectibles shop. It was right next to the Sanrio store.

jokes.text: David doesn't actually exist, does he? You're mad. Nothing you've said can prove that. Except that when we met you said his name was Andrew. Bollocks.
jokes.setup: Troy realizing Toby's stories don't add up
jokes.punchline: Toby's confident denial immediately undercut by Troy catching the name change
jokes.explanation: Great reveal moment - Troy showing unexpected detective skills while Toby's lie falls apart perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, you said your name was Steve last week.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: No I didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yes you did. You told me about when you were in the Peace Corps in Peru.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: That was... that was a different story.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, Toby—or should I say Steve—you literally introduced yourself as Steve.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I would never—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You're doing it right now. You're lying right now.

jokes.text: But maybe there's a reason the Inspector always chooses a human companion. He's an alien, but his human friends keep him grounded and invested in the world, like with me and Troy.
jokes.setup: Toby's speech about being superior to 'neurotypicals'
jokes.punchline: Abed using Inspector Spacetime philosophy to defend friendship
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment where Abed uses his pop culture knowledge to understand and defend real human connection.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'm superior to neurotypicals. My brain is simply more advanced.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Actually, Inspector Spacetime teaches us that true advancement isn't about being superior—it's about connection. The Inspector's greatest strength was his friendships across dimensions and species.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Your neurotype doesn't make you better or worse. It just makes you different. And difference is what makes a group stronger, not weaker.

jokes.text: Would you love me if I got you a churro?
jokes.setup: Toby trying to negotiate with kidnapped Abed
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt at Stockholm syndrome bribery
jokes.explanation: Shows Toby's desperation and social awkwardness - trying to win affection through snack food.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Look, Abed, I know this is a difficult situation, but I brought you a cappuccino from that place you like.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You kidnapped me.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I know, but I also got you a bagel. A fresh one. Still warm.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm calling the police.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Wait—what if I also throw in a movie night? We could watch whatever you want.

jokes.text: Because I have. Oh, my God. He can make a fist. That would hurt harder than a slap.
jokes.setup: Troy asking Toby if he's ever been in a fight
jokes.punchline: Toby's amazement at basic fist physics
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Troy's street knowledge and Toby's complete inexperience with physical confrontation.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Have you ever been in a fight?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: No! Why would I be in a fight?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I don't know, man. Just asking.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Well, I haven't. Fights are barbaric.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, but what if someone just... punched you?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'punched' me? Like with their fist?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: That's a closed hand moving at high velocity. That would hurt!

jokes.text: You know, for the first time in my long history of being locked inside things, I knew someone would come.
jokes.setup: Troy rescuing Abed from the box
jokes.punchline: Abed's casual reference to being frequently imprisoned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line - turning his history of being bullied into a touching moment about friendship trust.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Thanks for getting me out of the box, Troy. Though honestly, at this point, I've been in so many boxes I'm starting to think of them as my second home.

jokes.text: I saw actual hair that looked a lot like mine on my side of the sink, so I have some questions. First one, is that actually my hair, and, if so, did it fall out naturally? Because if it did, you need to tell me right now, 'cause I have to call science.
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering evidence of Annie's marriage fantasy
jokes.punchline: His vanity-driven panic about potential hair loss
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff moment - his vanity about his hair trumps everything else, including the weird marriage situation.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, you fantasize about marrying me? That's... actually kind of flattering.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: But here's what I'm concerned about — if we did get married, would I start losing my hair? Because I've seen what happens to married men. The stress, the responsibility...

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: My hair is basically my best feature. I can't risk that.

jokes.text: I've married you at least a half a dozen times. And Troy. And Zac Efron. Mostly Zac Efron.
jokes.setup: Annie admitting to daydreaming about marriage
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of fantasy husbands ending with the most frequent
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Annie's romantic fantasies with a funny escalation to celebrity crushes.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I've had fantasies about marrying different people. First it was Jeff, then Troy, then Abed, then Pierce, then Shirley, then the Dean, then Chang.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: But mostly just Abed.

jokes.text: If we were married, you wouldn't find me flirting with another woman in a hotel bar.
jokes.setup: Jeff critiquing Annie's marriage fantasy
jokes.punchline: His confident assertion about his faithfulness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's attempt at maturity while still being typically self-centered.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I would never cheat on you.

jokes.text: Oh, God, don't make me order that.
jokes.setup: Annie asking for an appletini
jokes.punchline: Jeff's horror at having to order a 'girly' drink
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff masculinity anxiety - his image concerns extend even to drink ordering.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Can I get an appletini?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: An appletini? Really? I'm not ordering that.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because I have to say the word 'appletini' out loud to a bartender.

jokes.text: Bow before Thoraxis.
jokes.setup: The group asking Jeff to perform the catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Jeff finally giving in and saying it
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the running gag - Jeff's character growth shown through embracing the group's nerdy interests.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Fine. Okay. Here it goes.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Cool. Cool cool cool.

jokes.text: See, this is why I don't hang with you folks on the weekends.
jokes.setup: Jeff performing the Thoraxis catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Shirley's matter-of-fact response to the weirdness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley moment - her practical, no-nonsense reaction to the group's absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Thoraxis!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I don't know what that is, and I'm not going to ask.

jokes.text: And now I must sleep with the sexiest woman here, who is also my grandmother, or I will cease to exist.
jokes.setup: Jeff doing an Inspector Spacetime impression in the tag
jokes.punchline: Extremely convoluted time travel logic involving incest
jokes.explanation: Good parody of convoluted sci-fi time travel plots with appropriately absurd stakes.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Inspector Spacetime here. I've just realized I traveled back in time and married my own grandmother, which means I'm my own grandfather. And also... my own father. And mother. And I may have... inadvertently become my own son.

jokes.text: I hate you.
jokes.setup: The absurd Inspector Spacetime roleplay
jokes.punchline: Britta's disgusted reaction to the whole scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta reaction - cutting through the absurdity with simple disgust.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is the dumbest thing I've ever seen.

tmdb_overview: Troy worries about losing Abed to a fellow Inspector Spacetime fan at a convention for the show, while Annie fantasizes about being married to Jeff.
insight: Meta-heavy episode mines cringe from convention cosplay, lands 49 jokes in 41 minutes.
